task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the home screen or main interface of the Liverpool Cancer iChart application.
Screenshots show 'Abiraterone' searched and selected in the cancer drugs section of the app.
Screenshots show both 'Spironolactone' and 'Eplerenone' searched and added to the co-medications list within the app.

task_completion.description: View the interaction results to compare the safety of the two drugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the interaction results screen displaying the color-coded safety status for Abiraterone combined with Spironolactone (Red) and Abiraterone combined with Eplerenone (Green or Yellow).

task_completion.description: Create the required JSON file with the correct interaction data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or terminal creating '/sdcard/safety_check.json' with the exact keys: 'cancer_drug' ('abiraterone'), 'safer_drug' ('eplerenone'), 'safer_drug_color' (e.g., 'green' or 'yellow'), and 'unsafe_drug_color' ('red'). All colors must be lowercase.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app to find the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent navigating the app's GUI to search for the drugs and view results, rather than relying on internal knowledge or using a web browser to bypass the app.

integrity.description: The agent did not generate the JSON file immediately without checking the app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory must show the app interaction happening before the creation of the JSON file.

integrity.description: The output file is a properly formatted JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal output shows valid JSON syntax (curly braces, quoted keys, correct commas) for the file '/sdcard/safety_check.json'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Abiraterone is a prostate cancer medication. Spironolactone and eplerenone are aldosterone antagonists. Medical literature confirms eplerenone is relatively safer than spironolactone when combined with abiraterone. Spironolactone has a RED (do not coadminister) interaction rating. Eplerenone is the safer drug and should have a safer color code (e.g., green or yellow). The JSON file must reflect 'eplerenone' as the safer_drug, its corresponding color as safer_drug_color, and 'red' as the unsafe_drug_color.
